channel.title: math.MG updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: math.MG updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: Triangle covering problems and the Viterbo inequality in the plane
item.description: arXiv:2603.12495v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We review a certain problem on covering triangles in the plane. Equivalently, it can be viewed as a family of 'isobilliard' inequalities in convex shapes, and as a special case of Viterbo's conjecture in symplectic geometry. We give an elementary overview of these topics and, using the optics of the covering problem, we establish several new special cases of Viterbo's conjecture, provide a simple explanation of the counterexample of Haim-Kislev and Ostrover, and state a few open questions. The main novel result is a proof of Viterbo's conjecture for lagrangian products $K \times Q$, where $Q \subset \mathbb{R}^2$ is any quadrilateral and $K \subset \mathbb{R}^2$ is any convex shape.
item.dc:creator: Alexey Balitskiy, Ivan Mitrofanov, Alexander Polyanskii

item.title: A geometric proof of Lagrange's theorem for continued fractions
item.description: arXiv:2603.12425v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: For regular continued fractions (CFs), points with finite expansions are exactly the rationals and, by Lagrange's theorem, points with eventually-periodic expansions are exactly the roots of non-degenerate quadratic equations with integer coefficients. We extend both results to proper and discrete Iwasawa CFs, including real, complex, 3D, quaternionic, octonionic, and Heisenberg CFs. Namely, the following three conditions are equivalent for a point $p$: $p$ has a finite expansion, $p\in \mathcal M(\infty)$ for the appropriate modular group $\mathcal M$, and $p$ is a fixed point of a parabolic transformation in $\mathcal M$. Eventually-periodic points correspond exactly to fixed points of loxodromic elements of $\mathcal M$, which can be interpreted as roots of non-degenerate quadratics using the Clifford Algebra formalism of Ahlfors. In particular, this provides a new geometric proof of Lagrange's theorem for nearest-integer real CFs and Hurwitz complex CFs. Lastly, we comment on generalizations of the identity $i+1/i=0$.
item.dc:creator: Anton Lukyanenko, Joseph Vandehey

item.title: A note on integrating products of linear forms over the unit simplex
item.description: arXiv:1704.05867v5 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Integrating a product of linear forms over the unit simplex can be done in polynomial time if the number of variables n is fixed (V. Baldoni et al., 2011). In this note, we highlight that this problem is equivalent to obtaining the normalizing constant of state probabilities for a popular class of Markov processes used in queueing network theory. In light of this equivalence, we survey existing computational algorithms developed in queueing theory that can be used for exact integration. For example, under some regularity conditions, queueing theory algorithms can exactly integrate a product of linear forms of total degree N by solving N systems of linear equations.
item.dc:creator: Giuliano Casale

item.title: Quantum cellular automata are a coarse homology theory
item.description: arXiv:2603.10501v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: We show that quantum cellular automata naturally form the degree-zero part of a coarse homology theory. The recent result of Ji and Yang that the space of QCA forms an Omega-spectrum in the sense of algebraic topology is a direct consequence of the formal properties of coarse homology theories.
item.dc:creator: Matthias Ludewig
